key_quote: The tool only supports fully-connected MLPs — no convolutions, attention, or normalization layers — limiting practical relevance to near-zero in 2024
rationale: The O(1/N²) finite-width correction with cross-layer accumulation term C_acc is acknowledged as a genuine theoretical contribution. But Theorem 1's proof is missing entirely, the tool is MLP-only (no practical relevance), the Bayesian classifier uses ad hoc heuristics, and bifurcation classification is incomplete. If implementation were perfected with Conv/attention support and the proof provided, reviewers would accept the novelty. This is Category B: novel idea, incomplete execution and scope.
